build_reproducibility.details: node build.js html produced byte-identical docs/index.html (1414.2 KB) vs published file

internal_anchors.details: All 836 href="#..." anchors resolve. 12 showTab() targets all match a ds-tab-content id. test.js Section 3 also clean.

tab_structure.details: Continuous chain: overview → evaluate → model → selftest → wins → pages → killshots → timestamp → predictions → falsify → ai → refs. First prev=null, last next=null. Heading hierarchy clean across all 12 tabs.

timeouts: https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/ (sandbox-side TLS/timeout — DNS resolves; published site reachable in real-world testing)
https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ (sandbox-side TLS EOF — github.io returns 301 redirect to this canonical URL; not a real outage)
external_links.details: Real broken link is the dome's own raw-text/03-predictions.txt (single occurrence in docs/index.html line 3007, sourced from sections.json). Carried forward from 2026-04-27 report. Spot-check shows the entire raw-text/* directory on dome (02-wins, 04-coordinates, 05-model, 06-evolution, 08-live-power) now also 404s — author has removed the raw-text mirror. 03-predictions.txt is the only one rendered as an actual <a href> in our HTML; the others are in-text references and don't generate broken links.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json length=71. Verdict bar in HTML uses 'X of 71 claims' with all six categories matching wins.json computed counts: Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Self-Contradicted 14, Refuted by Data 9, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sum=71). new_in_v51=32 matches HTML '+32' and '32 new WINs' breakdown. uncounted-failures.json entries=10 matches HTML '10 acknowledged failures'. '69 Wins Reviewed' = 69 unique base WIN numbers (71 entries minus the WIN-057b/058b paired sub-entries). llms.txt counts and meta tags align.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 WIN ids in wins.json have a matching id="winNNN" anchor in docs/index.html (69 base + 057b + 058b). Verdict color class (vt-refuted/vt-selfcon/vt-misleading/vt-std/vt-notdemo/vt-unfalsifiable) matches verdict text in every block (71/71). All 71 WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields populated.

win_dome_alignment.details: Dome wins.html has 69 unique WIN-NNN ids (WIN-001..WIN-069), all covered by our review. Dome page uses 72 h3-WIN occurrences — 3 ids appear twice with different titles (number-collision pattern, dome-side):

titles: WIN-057 — Two-Zone Disc Topology: Cross-Equatorial RMSE 6.2%
collisions.our_coverage: Two-zone disc topology (the confirmed-list version). PRED-R026 SAA acceleration is the dome's prospective-list reuse of the same number.

titles: WIN-058 — θ = −lonE: Unified Angular Coordinate Rule
collisions.our_coverage: Unified angular coordinate (the confirmed-list version). PRED-KAPPA-001 induction ratio is the dome's prospective-list reuse of the same number.
Tesla longitudinal wave speed — same claim, two phrasings. Benign.

win_dome_alignment.title_drift: None: every dome confirmed-list WIN-NNN matches our claim field.

progressive_disclosure.details: 334 details/summary blocks with ps-summary/ks-summary classes and populated ps-tldr/ks-tldr text (0 empty). 142 WIN-section details with ks-tldr (0 empty). All 13 genuinely_prospective predictions in predictions.json have a tldr field (13/13).

theme_colors.details: 0 hardcoded light backgrounds (#fff/#f8f9fa/etc.) in sections.json. 3 hardcoded color:#555 declarations (part4.html, part5.html, part6.html) — minor; consistent with previous reports' tolerance.

discoverability.details: docs/llms.txt (5628B) describes review accurately and references current 71-WIN/69-base count. docs/sitemap.xml is valid XML with both required <loc> entries (root + llms.txt). docs/robots.txt has Allow:/ + Sitemap:. <meta name=description>, og:title, og:description, and ClaimReview JSON-LD all present in <head>. Counts in meta tags ('69 claims', '0 survive', '10 acknowledged failures') match wins.json + uncounted-failures.json.

uncounted_failures_schema.details: 10 entries, 0 duplicate IDs, all required fields (id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened) present. Note: FAIL-006/007/008 use the pre-2026-04-09 schema (dome_label+summary) instead of the post-EXP-051 'prediction_name' field. _meta.schema_note already documents this transition; not a regression.

expansion_tracker.details: 271 items, next_id=274 > max_id=273 (OK, no collision imminent). 2 ID gaps (EXP-52 — file absent, likely intentional skip from early days; EXP-259 — file PRESENT but tracker entry missing).

orphans.issue: Output file with status:'ready-for-integration' exists in both git and FUSE (created 2026-04-26T12:06Z by analyst), but no EXP-259 entry in expansion-tracker.json. Decider will never integrate it.
orphans.suggested_fix: Analyst (or decider on next intake) needs to add an EXP-259 tracker entry pointing at this file, then mark integrated after applying the two SEC-1.8/WIN-016 quantitative fixes.

workspace_only_files.details: Same stray files flagged in 2026-04-27 report still present (no data-loss risk; cleanup recommended):
files: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (21KB DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW, 2026-04-22) — confirm draft content has been integrated, then delete .bak

workspace_sync_skips.details: 0 direction-violation skips and 0 mtime-guard skips in last 24h.

curmudgeon_digest_coverage.details: 12 unprocessed reviews (above the 10-threshold but explained by recent activity, not a filter regression). Unprocessed list is dominated by EXP-* proposal/section-new reviews from 2026-04-26 onward and one curmudgeon WIN-001.c5 (see digest_parse_errors below). All EXP-* prefixes are accepted by digest-reviews.js since the 2026-04-12 fix; this is normal pipeline lag awaiting the next decider run.

errors.diagnosis: Race between curmudgeon write and workspace-sync push: workspace-sync ran at 2026-04-28T08:05:07Z and committed an empty placeholder (commit 63d121d); curmudgeon then wrote the populated review at 2026-04-28T08:07:43Z. The workspace copy is valid JSON; git has an empty file. Will self-heal at next workspace-sync (~hourly cadence) — universal-pusher mode added 2026-04-26 should rescue. Until then, the digest pipeline reports a parse error and the decider cannot consume this WIN-001 review.

digest_parse_errors.auto_recovery: Wait <1h for next workspace-sync run (which will copy the populated FUSE WIN-001.c5.json back to git), or manually `cp "$WS/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-001.c5.json" "$GIT/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-001.c5.json"` then commit + push from the clean clone.

claude_md.details: Schedule table lists 8 agents; all 8 prompt files (analyst/curmudgeon/decider/poller/social/structure-integrity/tinker/workspace-sync) exist under monitor/prompts/. All 4 data files referenced (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json) exist. All 3 reference files agents read (SCIENTIFIC-CONTEXT.md, DATA-SCHEMAS.md, BUILD-AND-CHANGE.md) exist. No hardcoded counts detected (the 'WIN-NNN' is a placeholder in the WIN-onboarding flow text, not a count).

issues_found.description: Curmudgeon review WIN-001.c5.json is 0 bytes in git (workspace has 26433-byte valid JSON). Race between workspace-sync push (08:05:07Z) and curmudgeon write (08:07:43Z) committed the empty placeholder. Digest reports parse error; decider cannot consume the WIN-001 review until next sync corrects it.
issues_found.location: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-001.c5.json (in git only — workspace copy is fine)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Self-heals on next hourly workspace-sync. To fix immediately: `cp` the workspace's populated WIN-001.c5.json into a clean git clone and push.

issues_found.description: Orphaned analyst expansion: EXP-259-SEC1.8-WIN016-gaia-quantitative-fixes.json exists with status='ready-for-integration' (created 2026-04-26T12:06Z) but expansion-tracker.json has no EXP-259 entry. Decider will never integrate it — two arithmetic-correctness fixes (SEC-1.8 Gaia μas→degrees and WIN-016 'eight orders of magnitude' → 'more than six orders') are stalled.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Analyst (or decider on next intake) should add an EXP-259 entry to expansion-tracker.json pointing at this output file with status='ready-for-integration', then decider applies the two surgical substring patches and marks integrated.

issues_found.description: External link to dome's raw-text mirror returns 404. Spot-check shows the entire raw-text/* directory on the dome site (02-wins, 03-predictions, 04-coordinates, 05-model, 06-evolution, 08-live-power) now 404s — author appears to have removed the raw-text directory.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace the link with a current dome path (e.g. predictions.html anchor) or remove the raw-text mirror citation.

issues_found.description: Dome wins.html has number-collision pattern: WIN-057 and WIN-058 each appear twice with different titles (in addition to the previously-flagged WIN-062). Prospective-list versions (PRED-R026 SAA Simultaneous Acceleration; PRED-KAPPA-001 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio) reuse the same WIN-NNN numbers as the confirmed-list versions (Two-Zone Disc Topology; θ=−lonE Unified Angular Coordinate). Our review covers the confirmed-list versions; dome's '69 confirmed' count is inflated by these aliases.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Document this in our 'cross-reference aliases' tracking. Already partially documented for WIN-062 (per dome's 2026-04-17 registry_integrity_notes); WIN-057/058 are newer instances of the same pattern.

issues_found.description: Two stray workspace files persist (no data-loss risk but cleanup recommended): WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (0 bytes, 2026-04-21 cruft) and EXP-202.json.bak (21KB draft from 2026-04-22).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Delete the .build.js cruft. For the .bak: confirm draft content has been integrated into the live EXP-202, then delete.

issues_found.description: 3 hardcoded color:#555 declarations in sections.json (part4.html, part5.html, part6.html). Below threshold for moderate, but if a stylesheet utility class would do the job it should be added.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Optional: introduce a shared muted-text utility class in the stylesheet and replace the inline #555s.

summary: Build is reproducible (0-line diff), test suite is green (3,480 passed), all 12 tabs and the sectionNav chain are intact, all 836 internal anchors resolve, and data-prose consistency is clean across wins.json (71 entries / 69 base WINs), verdict tallies, new_in_v51 (32), uncounted-failures (10), and llms.txt counts. Two MAJOR issues require attention: (1) WIN-001.c5.json is 0 bytes in git due to a workspace-sync/curmudgeon write race (will self-heal at next sync, ~hourly), and (2) EXP-259-SEC1.8-WIN016-gaia-quantitative-fixes.json is a true orphan — completed analyst expansion sitting on disk with no tracker entry, blocking two correctness patches. The dome's raw-text/* mirror has been entirely removed (one broken link in our HTML); two new dome-side WIN-NNN aliases (057, 058) joined WIN-062 in the cross-reference pattern. Workspace stray files (.build.js, .bak) carry over from the 2026-04-27 report and should be swept.
